ddls:
CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the network service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network service */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the network service (e.g., Mandatory, Optional) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the service (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the service was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the service was last updated */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the service is hosted */,
  "cloud_environment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cloud environment where the service is hosted */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user responsible for the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network port */,
  "port_number" INTEGER /* Number of the network port */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the network port (e.g., TCP, UDP) */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network service using the port */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the network port (e.g., Mandatory, Optional) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the port (e.g., open, closed, filtered) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the port was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the port was last updated */,
  "last_accessed_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the port was last accessed */,
  "access_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the port has been accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ports_protocol FOREIGN KEY ("protocol") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ports_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the protocol was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the protocol was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service configuration */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network service */,
  "configuration_data" TEXT /* Configuration data for the service */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last updated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the configuration was created */,
  "updated_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who last updated the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_configurations_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_configurations_updated_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_security" (
  "security_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security policy */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network service */,
  "policy_data" TEXT /* Policy data for the service */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the policy was last updated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the security policy was created */,
  "updated_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who last updated the security policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("security_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_security_updated_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_security_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_monitoring" (
  "monitoring_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each monitoring data entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network service */,
  "monitoring_data" TEXT /* Monitoring data for the service */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the monitoring data was last updated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the monitoring data was created */,
  "updated_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who last updated the monitoring data */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of any alerts (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("monitoring_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_monitoring_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_monitoring_updated_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer, security analyst) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was last updated */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* User's active status (e.g., true, false) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network service being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the service */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., configure, monitor, secure) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the access log was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access attempt (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
